description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped dark stone with a rough, uneven surface and subtle white veining throughout. The stone appears dense and solid, with jagged edges and a generally asymmetrical form. One side features a patch of white paint or label with handwritten markings, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used for identification. The coloration is predominantly charcoal gray to black, with lighter streaks and occasional small patches of lighter mineral inclusions. The surface texture is coarse and matte, with visible micro-pitting and natural weathering. This stone would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A dense, dark gray stone with rough, jagged edges and subtle white veining, featuring a painted label on one side. Its coarse, matte surface and irregular shape make it a typical urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: The stone appears very thin from this angle, showing its narrowest edge and highlighting its irregular, jagged profile.
description_view_1: This view displays the broadest, flattest side of the stone, revealing its rough texture and subtle veining.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the stone's thinness and uneven contour.
description_view_3: This side shows the painted label with handwritten markings, set against the dark, rough surface of the stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle white veining and a painted label with handwritten markings; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and naturally weathered, with visible micro-pitting and minor surface abrasions.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by soil or mulch.
